mission.title: Install and Configure Oras on Kubernetes
mission.description: ORAS is an OCI registry client that allows you to manage content like artifacts, images, and packages. Installing ORAS enables you to interact with OCI-compliant registries effectively.

steps.title: Add the ORAS Helm repository
steps.description:
To install ORAS using Helm, first add the ORAS Helm repository:
```bash
helm repo add oras https://oras.land/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install ORAS using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install ORAS with the following command, specifying the version and creating a namespace:
```bash
helm install oras oras/oras --version v1.3.0 --namespace oras --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ORAS pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oras
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the ORAS deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment oras --namespace oras
``` 
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Access ORAS CLI
steps.description:
To use the ORAS CLI, you can port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/oras --namespace oras 8080:80
``` 
You can now access the ORAS CLI at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ORAS pods running in the 'oras' namespace. You can verify the installation by checking the pod status and accessing the ORAS CLI through port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oras https://oras.land/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install oras oras/oras --version v1.3.0 --namespace oras --create-namespace

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
